gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Empty-day reward rollup into penaltiesInRewardPool allows first staker after inactive period to claim accumulated base rewards beyond entitlement
candidate.claimed_root_cause: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool unconditionally rolls rewardPool[d] into penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] for every past day where totalShares[d]==0, then calculateStakingRewards distributes the entire accumulated penaltiesInRewardPool to whoever holds shares on currentDay
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker staking for 1 day after N days of inactivity receives ~N days of base reward emissions via penaltiesInRewardPool, minted by torus.mint

live_preconditions.name: At least one past day with totalShares[d]==0 exists (inactive period)
live_preconditions.observed_value: Not directly observable from live_context; totalShares mapping not enumerated. Protocol started 1752170400 (~July 2025); realistic that early days or any gap period had zero stakers.

live_preconditions.name: rewardPool[1] initialized to INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD
live_preconditions.observed_value: Confirmed in constructor at line 142: rewardPool[1] = INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD (100_000e18)

live_preconditions.name: Attacker can call createTorus (has TitanX or ETH for fee)
live_preconditions.observed_value: createTorus is a public payable function; no role restriction. TitanX available on mainnet.

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: For every past day d where totalShares[d]==0 and d!=currentDay, the entire rewardPool[d] (nextPool) and any existing penaltiesInRewardPool[d] are unconditionally added to penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay]. No check that the current staker was present during those days.

Both rewardPool[day] and penaltiesInRewardPool[day] are distributed pro-rata by shares. The function has no concept of whether penaltiesInRewardPool originated from actual penalties or from rolled-up empty-day base rewards.
Calls _checkAndUpdateRewardPool() then calculateStakingRewards then torus.mint(msg.sender, totalAmount). No eligibility check tying claimant to origin of penaltiesInRewardPool.
rewardPool[1] = INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD = 100_000e18 seeds the rolling accumulation.
DAILY_REDUCTION_RATE=8 BPS and INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD=100_000e18 define the magnitude of per-day rollup.

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA with TitanX (or ETH) sufficient to pay createTorus fee
call_sequence: 1. Wait N days from protocol start with zero protocol interactions (totalShares[d]==0 for d in [2..N])

2. Call createTorus(1, 1) on day N+1: triggers _checkAndUpdateRewardPool(), which loops d=2..N+1; for each d in [2..N] with totalShares[d]==0, adds rewardPool[d] to penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1]; attacker becomes sole holder of totalShares[N+1]

4. Call claim(0): calculateStakingRewards iterates day N+1 only; attacker holds 100% of totalShares[N+1]; receives rewardPool[N+1] + penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1] (= sum of rewardPool[2..N]); torus.mint delivers full amount
state_after: attacker receives principal + rewardPool[N+1] + sum(rewardPool[2..N]) minted TORUS

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: For N=30: ~29 * ~98,800e18 ≈ 2,865,200 TORUS minted to attacker in addition to single-day entitlement of ~98,800 TORUS
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol inflation: TORUS supply inflated by ~N-1 days of base emissions that should have remained unallocated or been distributed to legitimate future stakers; no existing staker is directly drained but total supply is inflated beyond schedule
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker staked for exactly 1 day (day N+1) and is entitled only to rewardPool[N+1]. Instead they receive rewardPool[N+1] plus the entire accumulated penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1] which contains base emissions from days 2..N — days during which the attacker held zero shares and had no stake.

setup: Deploy mock Torus token with mint authority granted to TorusCreateAndStake
Deploy mock TitanX ERC20; fund attacker
vm.warp(protocolStart + 30 days) to simulate 30 days of inactivity
expected_assertions: assertGt(penaltiesAccumulated, 0) // penaltiesInRewardPool[31] contains rolled-up base rewards
assertGt(attackerBalance, createAndStake.rewardPool(31) * 20) // attacker received >20x single-day entitlement
// Invariant violation: penaltiesInRewardPool should only contain penalty funds from late/early claims, not base emissions
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If penaltiesInRewardPool[31] == 0 after createTorus call, or if attacker balance <= rewardPool[31] alone, the vulnerability is not present.
